title: Cache Book Titles with Redis
statement: Implement a simple REST API to cache book titles in Redis. The API should accept a JSON payload with a book ID and title. On GET, it should return the cached title for the given ID. If the title is not cached, it should be fetched from a static JSON file and cached before being returned. If the GET request is made with a specific query parameter, it should clear the cache for that book ID. You should use Express.js for your API and Redis as the cache store. Note: You will need to install the 'redis' package and import it in your code. You can assume that Redis is running on the default port at `localhost:6379`.
constraints: Must use the 'redis' package to interact with Redis.
Must fetch titles from a static JSON file if not cached.

endpoints.description: Get the cached title for a book ID. If the title is not cached, it will be fetched from the static JSON file and cached.
Get the cached title for a book ID and clear the cache for that ID. If the title is not cached, it will be fetched from the static JSON file, cached, and then cleared.
Cache a new book title. The request body should contain the book ID and title.
